name: python-copy-qualified-name
description: Copy fully qualified Python method and function names to clipboard

pythonCopyQualifiedName.fileMonitor.enab.description: Enable monitoring of open Python files

pythonCopyQualifiedName.fileMonitor.chec.description: Interval in minutes to check for open Python files

pythonCopyQualifiedName.fileMonitor.maxF.description: Maximum number of Python files that should be open before showing a warning

pythonCopyQualifiedName.hostScripts.host.description: Hostname of the host task server. Leave empty to auto-detect (localhost on host, host.docker.internal in devcontainer)

pythonCopyQualifiedName.hostScripts.port.description: Port the host task server listens on

pythonCopyQualifiedName.hostScripts.host.description: The host-side filesystem path of this workspace. Required when running inside a devcontainer (e.g. /Users/you/src/project). Leave empty when on the host.

enumDescriptions: Update the workspace window title and title bar colors by writing workspace settings.
Show the worktree identity in a session-only status bar item without modifying workspace files.
Do not show the worktree identity.
pythonCopyQualifiedName.worktreeIdentity.description: Controls how worktree identity is shown. Use statusBar to avoid Git-visible workspace setting changes.
Template used for the VS Code window title when worktree identity mode is workspaceSettings. Supports the additional ${worktreeName} placeholder.
Saturation used for the worktree title bar color. Hue is still derived from the worktree name hash.
Lightness used for the worktree title bar color when the active theme is light.
Lightness used for the worktree title bar color when the active theme is dark.

languageModelTools.name: doctorc_list_vscode_tasks
languageModelTools.userDescription: List VS Code tasks available in the current workspace window.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Lists VS Code tasks visible to the current VS Code extension host/window. Use this before running a VS Code task when the exact task label or task type is unclear.

type.description: Optional VS Code task type filter, such as shell or hostScript.

languageModelTools.name: doctorc_run_vscode_task
languageModelTools.userDescription: Run a VS Code task by label in the current workspace window.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Runs a VS Code task from the current VS Code extension host/window using vscode.tasks.executeTask, preserving VS Code task resolution, dependencies, inputs, environment, host/container task providers, and workspace scope. Use only when the user asks to run, start, launch, or execute a VS Code task.

label.description: Exact VS Code task label/name to run.

type.description: Optional VS Code task type filter, such as shell or hostScript.

workspaceFolder.description: Optional absolute workspace folder path. Use this to disambiguate same-label tasks in multi-root windows.

command.description: Shell command to execute on the host machine

args.description: Extra arguments passed to the command

options.description: Execution options for the host task

cwd.description: Working directory for the host task

env.description: Environment variables for the host task

commands.title: Host Scripts: Run Task by Label

scripts.test: npm run compile && python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py'
